zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Burdon於大約1455年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾時，她的父親John Burdon23歲，母親Elizabeth Millicent DeBeckering Bekeryne19歲。她於大約1465年與Francis Pierrepont結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1506年在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾霍爾梅 皮朋特享年52歲。

nameMeanings.name: Burdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Polish: nickname for a troublemaker (see   Burda  ).

variants.name: Burden
meanings: (southern England, of Norman origin): from a diminutive of the ancient Germanic personal name Burdo, for which compare Burdett . Burdonus and other derivatives of the Gaulish personal name Burdo (‘mule’) are found in France, where it is rare and the similar French surname may derive instead from Old French bourdon in the sense ‘pilgrim's staff’ (compare Bourdon 1). The personal name, whether of ancient Germanic or Gaulish origin, does occur in medieval England, but rarely.

habitational name from any of various places called Burdon or Burden. Burden in Yorkshire and Great Burdon in County Durham are named with Old English burh ‘stronghold, fortified place’ + dūn ‘hill’; Burdon in Tyne and Wear is named with Old English b̄re ‘byre’ + denu ‘valley’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Burdon was born about 1455, in Nottinghamshire, England, her father, John Burdon, was 23 and her mother, Elizabeth Millicent DeBeckering Bekeryne, was 19. She married Francis Pierrepont about 1465. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1506, in Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Burdon est née environ 1455, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, John Burdon, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Millicent DeBeckering Bekeryne, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Francis Pierrepont environ 1465. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1506, à Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Burdonが約1455年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父John Burdonは23歳，母Elizabeth Millicent DeBeckering Bekeryneは19歳であった。約1465年にFrancis Pierrepontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1506年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアホールメ ピエレプオントで52歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Burdon(이)가 대략 1455년 영국 노틴그함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Burdon(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Millicent DeBeckering Bekeryne(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 대략 1465년 Francis Pierrepont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1506년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 홀 피레폰트에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Burdon родилась около 1455 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Burdon было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Millicent DeBeckering Bekeryne, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Francis Pierrepont около 1465 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1506 г. в местности холм пьиррепонт, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 52 лет.
